title: CIS-3000
short description: CIS-3000 is an 8-PSK Data Modem protocol. Its source is traced to Russia. 3000 is for its 3000 Baud speed (maximum speed is technically 9000 bps).
description:
**CIS-3000** is an 8-PSK Data Modem protocol. Its source is traced to Russia. 3000 is for its 3000 Baud speed (maximum speed is technically 9000 bps). 

CIS-3000 has been seen acting like [2G ALE](https://www.sigidwiki.com/wiki/Automatic Link Establishment (2G ALE)), where it has been seen to prelude [CIS-128](https://www.sigidwiki.com/wiki/CIS-128) transmissions and [CIS MFSK-68](https://www.sigidwiki.com/wiki/CIS MFSK-68) transmissions like a calling mode. 

Data on this signal is found in Roland Proesch's *Technical Handbook for Radio Monitoring HF*.
